channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ghana's Partey loses bid to overturn Canadian visa refusal over rape charges
item.description: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey lost a court challenge on Tuesday that would have allowed him to enter Canada for his side's World Cup opener in Toronto. The former Arsenal player, who awaits trial on rape charges in Britain, will be eligible to play in Ghana's following two matches, both of them in the United States/
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France puts 14 on trial over deadliest Channel migrant crossing disaster
item.description: Fourteen people will stand trial in France over the deaths of 31 migrants in the deadliest recorded small-boat disaster in the English Channel, prosecutors said on Tuesday. The defendants are accused of links to people-smuggling networks involved in the November 2021 sinking, which left four others missing.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: French supporters go wild after victory against Senegal
item.description: After a slow and uninspired start, France finally managed to kick off their 2026 World Cup with a win, defeating Senegal 3-1. French supporters watching the game from the Paris fan zone went wild at Kylian Mbappe's two goals. The French football star is now the new all-time leading scorer for the French national team.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mbappé becomes France's record scorer as Les Bleus down valiant Senegal
item.description: Favourites France overcame a wobbly start to defeat Group I rivals Senegal 3-1 in their World Cup opener on Tuesday, with captain Kylian Mbappé scoring twice to overtake Olivier Giroud as Les Bleus' all-time top scorer and move within two goals of Miroslav Klose's World Cup record. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes reports from the MetLife stadium in New Jersey. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports from Paris where French and Senegalese supporters were gathering in the French capital's fan zones.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: FBI foils plot to attack White House UFC event using explosive drones, snipers
item.description: US law enforcement said on Tuesday it had foiled an alleged plot to attack the White House during a mixed martial arts event attended by President Donald Trump. Five men were charged after authorities disrupted plans to deploy explosive-laden drones and use snipers to target “high-value targets” amid an evacuation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Who is Vozinha, heroic goalkeeper of Cape Verde?
item.description: Goalkeeper Vozinha was Cape Verde's hero in Monday's 0-0 draw against Spain, which marked his country's first-ever World Cup match. At age 40, he was playing in his first World Cup match and was named man of the match.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Senegal lose 3-1 to France in second-half smasher
item.description: In tonight's edition, Senegal faces two-time champions France in New Jersey in a kickoff for the group of death at the World Cup. Also, Africa CDC warns the Ebola crisis in Congo has the potential to become the worst ever of its kind. And 50 years ago, thousands of young south africans from Soweto protested against apartheid's stranglehold on their education and the imposition of Afrikaans as a language of instruction.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: Can Hormuz be bypassed? Past shows commercial viability may simply not be there, analyst says
item.description: On Tuesday, leaders at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains were seeking to identify alternative routes to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, which US President Donald Trump said would be "completely open" on Friday. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Maisoon H Kafafy, Senior Advisor to the Atlantic Council’s Middle East programme, says that in previous instances of oil supply disruptions "the commercial viability [of building alternative infrastructure] was simply not there and the economics of the upfront investment into this projects were perhaps too large to justify".
item.dc:creator: Sharon GAFFNEY

item.title: Did Macron ‘snub’ Trump at the G7 summit? Viral video lacks context
item.description: With the G7 summit beginning Tuesday in France, social media users claimed French leader Emmanuel Macron deliberately "humiliated" Donald Trump by not being there to greet him upon his arrival at the airport. Many posts shared a video showing Trump disembarking an aircraft and later appearing unsmiling during a handshake with Macron, suggesting the two events were linked. However, the viral footage omits important context.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL

item.title: World Cup 2026: France beat Senegal as Mbappé becomes Les Bleus' record goal-scorer
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé becomes France's record scorer as Les Bleus down valiant Senegal
item.description: Favourites France overcame a wobbly start to defeat Group I rivals Senegal 3-1 in their World Cup opener on Tuesday, with captain Kylian Mbappé scoring twice to overtake Olivier Giroud as Les Bleus' all-time top scorer and move within two goals of Miroslav Klose's World Cup record.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump suggests US can swiftly reimpose sanctions on Russian oil
item.description: As the war in Ukraine took centre stage at the G7 summit in Evian, France, US President Donald Trump said Moscow should make peace with Kyiv and suggested that the US could bring back sanctions on Russian oil now that the Strait of Hormuz is expected to reopen. Also in the segment, the iconic BHV department store in Paris has announced it will end its partnership with ultra-fast-fashion giant Shein, as it changes ownership.
item.dc:creator: Yuka ROYER

item.title: Russian warship fires 'warning shots' at UK yacht in Channel
item.description: Russia said one of its warships fired warning shots Tuesday at a yacht making a "dangerous approach" in the Channel, as the UK said it was investigating the incident. It involved the frigate Admiral Grigorovich and a UK-registered yacht around 20 nautical miles south of the Isle of Wight just outside British waters. FRANCE 24's Bénédicte Paviot reports from London.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: SOS messages from Mideast seas plunge India-US ties into choppy waters
item.description: The US military killed three Indian soldiers last week when it fired on a vessel while enforcing a US naval blockade, the latest in a series of setbacks for US-India ties. As Prime Minister Narendra Modi meets President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the G7 summit this week, the two leaders will seek to reset bilateral relations. But for Modi, it might be too little, too late.
item.dc:creator: Leela JACINTO

item.title: Trump 'doesn't share European view about Russian threat', analyst says
item.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ian Lesser, Distinguished Fellow and Advisor to the President of the German Marshall Fund of the United States, explains that Trump "doesn't completely share the European view about the nature of the threat to European security that Russian behaviour poses. Fot him this is a conflict to be gotten over, to be moved off the table and if Europe can take the lead in doing that, so much the better".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: JR's "The Cave" on Paris' Pont Neuf is finally open to the public
item.description: "The Cave" at Pont Neuf has finally opened its doors this Monday. The announcement was made by the artist JR in the afternoon, as repairs were nearing completion after the canvas was torn by strong winds on June 2. It is now possible to explore, free of charge and 24 hours a day, the interior of the ephemeral artwork by the artist who became famous for his monumental photographic collage projects and trompe-l'oeil.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé brace ignites France's title bid after sleepy start against Senegal
item.description: World Cup favourites France rebounded from a poor first half to beat Senegal 3-1 in their Group I opener on Tuesday, with captain Kylian Mbappé scoring twice to move past Pelé and Lionel Messi with 14 World Cup goals – and become Les Bleus' all-time record scorer. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: Ukraine back in favour? At G7, Trump threatens return of Russia oil sanctions
item.description: Before he left Washington, there were reports Volodymyr Zelenskiy wasn't even on Donald Trump's G7 bilateral meeting list, but in the end, the U-S president took the meeting. And more. In the French Alps resort of Evian, Trump putting the onus on Vladimir Putin to come to the bargaining table and even suggesting a rapid return to full sanctions on Russian oil. Why the sudden turn-for-the-better in relations between Washington and Kyiv?
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Russian warship fires warning shots at UK-flagged yacht in the English Channel
item.description: The British military said it is investigating after a Russian warship fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday, about 30 kilometres south of the Isle of Wight. Russia said one of its warships fired warning shots after it made a "dangerous approach".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pont Neuf 'Cavern' installation over the Seine finally opens after damage
item.description: Visitors are finally stepping into darkness at the Pont Neuf, where French artist JR has transformed Paris’s oldest bridge into a towering artificial cavern. The installation was originally set to open to the public on June 6, but strong winds tore parts of the inflatable structure a few days before its début. Combining scent, sound, photography and augmented reality, the free installation invites people to experience one of the city’s most familiar landmarks in an entirely new way.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Super powers in the 21st century 'often not able' to defeat smaller powers
item.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Peter Zalmayev, Director of the Eurasia Democracy Initiative. His central argument is that traditional assumptions about military strength are increasingly being challenged by technological innovation, asymmetric warfare, and political resilience. In this framework, Ukraine's ability to strike deep into Russian territory and sustain resistance against a much larger adversary serves as evidence that military size alone no longer guarantees strategic success. He suggests that the conflict's ultimate resolution will depend less on battlefield maps than on political calculations in Moscow, Washington, and Kyiv, as well as on the ability of the international community to create a durable framework for Ukrainian sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Iran: Uncertainty over Iran deal looms large at G7 summit
item.description:
🇺🇸🇮🇷 The question on everyone’s mind…
Blick's Richard Werly tells #F24Debate uncertainty over the contents of Trump's #Iran agreement looms large at the Évian #G7summit ➡️ https://go.france24.com/vZZZ
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In post-Assad Syria, displaced people await reconstruction
item.description: Syria has been particularly hard hit by the decline in global humanitarian aid, especially from the US. In the northwest of the country, hundreds of thousands of displaced people are still living in makeshift shelters. When the Assad regime fell, they hoped to be able to return home. But on seeing their villages destroyed, most have remained stranded in camps, waiting for reconstruction that will take years. As time goes by, living conditions there are deteriorating and vital infrastructure such as hospitals are at risk of closing.
item.dc:creator: Dana ALBOZ
